stemHtml: Which of the following is correct regarding the stall speed?

options.a: When flying in icing conditions and there is ice buildup on the wings, the stall speed is unknown but almost always greater than the clean stall speed.
options.b: For an aircraft experiencing spiralling slipstream over the tail, the stall speed will be higher due to the nose-up pitching moments.
options.c: On multi-engine aeroplanes, the upwards pitching moment caused by asymmetric thrust will lead to an increase in the stall speed.
options.d: A single engine aeroplane subject to the torque effect will experience an increase in the stall speed due to the vortex created by the propeller wash.
